---
title: ""
output: 
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
date: ""
---

```{r setup, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

library(tidycensus)
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)

# grab income data

turnout_all <- readRDS("data/turnout_all.rds")

income_data <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = "B19013_001",
  state = c("WI", "MI", "IL", "OH", "IN"),
  year = 2024
)

# clean it 

income_clean <- income_data |>
  select(GEOID, estimate) |>
  rename(geoid = GEOID, median_income = estimate)

turnout_income <- turnout_all |>
  left_join(income_clean, by = "geoid")
```

```{r add quintile, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
il_income <- turnout_income %>%
  filter(state == "IL") %>%
  mutate(income_quartile = ntile(median_income, 4),
         income_group = case_when(
           income_quartile == 1 ~ "Lowest",
           income_quartile == 2 ~ "Low-Middle",
           income_quartile == 3 ~ "High-Middle",
           income_quartile == 4 ~ "Highest"
         ),
         income_group = factor(income_group, levels = c("Lowest", "Low-Middle", "High-Middle", "Highest")))

```

```{r plot, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=3.5, out.width="100%"}
il_income_avg <- il_income %>%
  group_by(income_group) %>%
  summarise(avg_turnout = mean(turnout_rate, na.rm = TRUE))
p <- ggplot(il_income_avg, aes(x = income_group, y = avg_turnout,
                                fill = avg_turnout)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#c8e0f0", high = "#1a3a5c") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.55, 0.75)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Georgia"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#faf9f6", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#faf9f6", color = NA)
  )
p
```
